name: OrgX Brand Pack
northStar: Instrument-grade matte control surface where the OrgX gradient is EMISSIVE light, never glossy reflection. Resolves the tension between the luminous 3D mark and the matte product system: the world is machined and dark; light is the one reserved reward, and it glows from within.

notes.markSource: The repository contains PNG masters for the OrgX mark, but no native vector source file for the mark itself.
notes.svgStatus: orgx-mark.svg is an SVG wrapper around the canonical PNG mark. orgx-wordmark.svg is a true SVG path export built from the repo's Geist font.
notes.v2: v1 (orgx-logo-pack) was a logo-asset manifest. v2 adds materiality, depth, light behavior, motion, and the UI-vs-film grain distinction required for brand-true motion/film work. Asset paths preserved from v1.

materialResolution.principle: Matte world + emissive accent. The mark's gloss is honored as LUMINESCENCE (edge-light, underglow, the gradient line, the gate bloom), not as glossy SaaS plastic.
materialResolution.world: matte machined dark — dark-aluminum / smoked-glass / precision-cut bezels; flat, never gradient-washed
materialResolution.accentBehavior: emitted, not reflected — light pools, glowing seams, inner-glow; appears only as signal/identity/reward
materialResolution.heroObject: the OrgX mark is the ONE permitted glossy, dimensional, specular artifact — used sparingly, like a hero product render

signatureGradient.role: RESERVED reward energy — the only place warm-lime→teal→cyan appears; it is light, not fill
signatureGradient.angle: diagonal, mark-knot direction (yellow top-left → cyan bottom-right)

color.reservedRewardForFilm: teal→cyan emissive — appears NOWHERE until the gate-pass peak; red (#F43F5E) appears exactly once (the leak)

layers: page void (deepest, subtle 20px grid + radial primary glow)
depthModel.rule: shallow deliberate depth — no elevation soup, no stacked cards, no floating badges

light.edgeLight: 1px gradient seam across card top (the signature OrgX detail) — linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, rgba(primary,0.5), transparent)
light.gridGlow: radial-gradient(circle at 50% 0%, rgba(primary,0.08), transparent 60%) over a 20px matte grid
light.underglow: for film: a soft pool of the reserved gradient beneath the hero card (EPTA WOLTAO/Torque move) — emissive, diffuse, low
light.bloomPolicy: bloom + contrast spike ONLY on the peak frame (gate pass); world stays matte and even elsewhere

voices.label: mono, 0.55-0.65rem, 600-700, +0.08-0.14em, uppercase, muted
voices.hero: Geist, 140-280px display for film headlines, tracking -1 to -3

typography.filmHeadline: Geist display, brutal scale contrast (hero word >=6:1 vs label), mask/clip reveal not opacity fade

motion.personality: clinical — fast attack, smooth settle; NO bounce, NO overshoot on data, NO confetti
motion.exception: Process/scaffold surfaces only: controlled emergence (orb drift, sparkle) — the one place OrgX celebrates

uiVsFilm.ui: matte, NO grain, NO noise, NO glossy fills, clinical motion, surveillance-calm — per philosophy.md
uiVsFilm.film: SAME matte world, but a cinematic grade LAYER on top: 2-5% film grain, vignette, faint chromatic aberration at edges, bloom on the peak — to defeat the clean-vector AI look. Grain lives in the film grade, never in shipped UI.
uiVsFilm.rule: the product UI shown inside the film must remain pixel-real and matte; the grain/bloom are a post layer over the whole frame, not a UI texture

filmMaterialDirection.void: near-black #02040A, depth haze, subtle machined grid barely visible
filmMaterialDirection.cards: smoked-glass / dark-aluminum, precision bezels, matte faces, 1px emissive edge-light seam
filmMaterialDirection.accentLight: lime->teal->cyan underglow pooling beneath the hero card; emissive, never a glossy reflection
filmMaterialDirection.heroMark: the glossy 3D OrgX knot as the one specular object — reserved for the lock/peak
filmMaterialDirection.negativePrompt_for_genVideo: no text, no glossy plastic, no rainbow, no neon clutter, no lens flare spam, no warped UI glyphs, no busy background

usage.reservedColorDiscipline: the lime-teal-cyan gradient is signal/reward only — never decorative headers, never tinted backgrounds, never accent section labels
